choices.text: 25 steps. Default safety against infinite loops. Override per invocation: `app.invoke(input, {'recursion_limit': 50})`. Track iteration count in state for custom logic. Always have termination conditions (task complete, max iter, quality threshold, resource exhausted). Production safety: max iter + cost cap + timeout to prevent runaway costs/behavior. Without limits, buggy agents can loop indefinitely racking up API costs.

questions.explanation: 25 steps default. Configurable per invocation. Always have termination.

questions.prompt: Agent loop runs indefinitely; never finishes. Debug?

choices.text: Check termination logic + add max iteration tracking. Common causes: 1) Conditional edge always routes back to agent; never to END. 2) State doesn't progress (same input each iteration). 3) Tool keeps returning same result; agent keeps trying. Fix: 1) Verify should_continue logic actually returns END on done conditions. 2) Track iterations in state; force END after threshold. 3) Inspect state changes per iteration (LangSmith visual debugger). 4) Log node executions to see loop structure. 5) Add quality check that breaks if output stable. Always have multiple termination paths: task done, max iter, time limit, error condition. Production agents NEVER have only one termination path.

questions.explanation: Check termination logic, add iteration tracking, use LangSmith debugger. Multiple termination paths essential.
